findings.evidence: `JHolomorphicEnergy.lean:69` and `:88` quantify over `{U}` and `{F₀ : U →ₗ[ℝ] V}`; `JHolomorphicLine.lean:148` already contains the adjacent generic `IsComplexLinearMap` symplectic-area lemma.
findings.fix: Move both generic lemmas to `TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphicLine.lean` near `symplecticForm_apply_apply_nonneg`/`symplecticForm_apply_apply_pos`, leaving only the standard-line energy definition and consequences here.
findings.issue: `associatedBilinForm_apply_apply_self_eq` and `associatedBilinForm_apply_self_eq_symplecticForm` are generic `IsComplexLinearMap` lemmas for any source `U`, not energy-density or standard-line declarations, so placing them in `JHolomorphicEnergy` forces later users to import the energy layer for basic complex-linear algebra.

findings.evidence: The grep hits in this file reference `SymplecticForm.associatedBilinForm_apply` and `ω.Tames J`, defined in `AlmostComplex.lean:319` and `:324`; there are no references to declarations introduced in `CompatibleMetric.lean`.
findings.fix: Drop `public import TauCeti.Geometry.Symplectic.CompatibleMetric`; if one of the moved generic lemmas needs a narrower dependency, import that at its new home instead.
findings.issue: The `CompatibleMetric` import is evidently unused by the declarations in this file; the file uses `associatedBilinForm`, `Tames`, and standard-line/J-holomorphic APIs already available through `JHolomorphicLine` and its `AlmostComplex` import, and no `CompatibleMetric` declaration is referenced.

summary: The new energy file is mostly in the right topic area, but it hides generally useful complex-linear associated-bilinear-form lemmas behind a later energy module and carries an evidently unnecessary import.
